description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline with stages build, test, and deploy, using the default node:latest image and the docker:stable-dind service for security jobs. In the build stage define build_commonjs, build_docs, build_es6, and build_quality jobs that each cache the node_modules directory per commit (key ${CI_COMMIT_REF_SLUG}), exclude schedules, run only on master, tags (and development for build_commonjs), execute 'yarn install' then 'yarn build:common', 'yarn build:docs', 'yarn build:es6', or 'yarn build:quality' respectively, and publish 1‑hour artifacts named $CI_JOB_NAME-$CI_COMMIT_REF_NAME-* containing lib/common, docs/, lib/es6, or quality/. In the test stage add test_commonjs_node_10, test_commonjs_node_6, test_commonjs_node_8, and test_commonjs_node_latest jobs that pull the same node_modules cache, depend on build_commonjs, run only on master and tags (latest also on development), use node:10, node:6, node:8, and node:latest images, run 'yarn install' and 'yarn test' (the latest also runs nyc with exclusions), and the latest job stores .nyc_output/ as an artifact. In the deploy stage add a code_coverage job (node:latest) that allows failure, depends on test_commonjs_node_latest, runs ' ./node_modules/.bin/nyc report --reporter="text-summary" --reporter="lcov"', caches node_modules, and uploads coverage/lcov-report/ as a 1‑day artifact; add a code_quality job (docker:latest) that allows failure, sets SP_VERSION from CI_SERVER_VERSION, runs a Docker container of registry.gitlab.com/gitlab-org/security-products/codequality:$SP_VERSION with the source mounted and docker socket, uses the docker:stable-dind service and DOCKER_DRIVER=overlay2, and runs only on master and tags; add a dependency_scanning job (docker:latest) with similar SP_VERSION logic, runs the dependency‑scanning image, also allows failure, uses the same service and variable, and uploads gl-dependency-scanning-report.json. Finally, add a publish_npm job in the deploy stage that depends on all build jobs, runs only on tags and triggers, writes the NPM auth token to ~/.npmrc, and publishes the package with 'npm publish --scope=@openrailuk --access=public', excluding schedules.
